page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, featuring 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance does not include local ephemeral storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark results place the E20s_v5 in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The processor achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1897.48 and a multi-core score of 19310.24, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 25434.63. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1042.98 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 60.71 GB/sec. In database operations, the server demonstrated a PassMark database score of 9828.91 and processed 19.46 million Redis SET operations per second. For language model tasks, it managed 43.9 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model, though performance drops significantly to 4.22 tokens per second on a large 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20s_v5 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of bundled local storage, requiring reliance on external storage volumes. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, featuring 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows solid mid-range performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 25434.63, a Redis SET throughput of 19.46 million operations per second, and a PassMark database score of 9828.91. The instance is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications where high memory density is required but local ephemeral storage is not necessary.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for relational databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 160 GB of RAM, designed for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding database and caching workloads.

160.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
No local ephemeral storage, utilizing remote Premium Storage instead
Delivers 19.46 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
